(916) 322·5662 
Louise T. Closs 
City Attorney 
City of Needles 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/Legal 
322·5901 
December 5, 1984 
Enforcement 
322-6«1 
Statements of Economic Intere" 
322-6«4 
c/o Law Offices of Closs & Closs 
P.O. Box 1070 
Needles, CA 
92363 
Dear Ms. Closs: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice; 
Our No. A-84-294 
We are in receipt of your letter requesting advice from 
this agency regarding the possible incompatibility of public 
offices arising from the Mayor's wife serving as the Chair of 
the City's Planning Commission. 
At present, our agency is not authorized to render advice 
on this subject, as the Political Reform Act deals only with 
conflicts between official duties and private financial 
interests. 
For advice on possible conflicts arising from two 
public offices, you should contact the Attorney General. 
I 
enclose a copy of our November 1983 Bulletin, which contains a 
listing of several Attorney General's Opinions on the subject 
(at page 4). 
REL:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
r"--:; 
YL11'--l~ ,-1--
~bert E. ~~9h 7 
Counsel . 
Legal Division 


JOHN D. CLOSS 
Admitted in California 
Arizona 
LOUISE T. CLOSS 
Admitted in California 
LAW OFFICES OF CLOSS & CLOSS 
121 "F" STREET 
PARK PLAZA PROFESSIONAL BUIWING 
SUITE 2 
P.O. BOX 1070 
NEEDLES, CAUFORNIA 92363 
(619) 326-2660 
November 20, 1984 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 "K" Street 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Subject: 
Conflict of Interest 
TORRANCE OFFICE 
18411 S. Crenshaw Boulevard 
Suite 403 
Torrance, California 90504 
(213) 532-4162 
The Needles City Council and the Mayor have requested an 
opinion regarding the following issue: 
"May the Mayor's wife serve as a member 
of the Planning Commission?" 
The facts are as follows: 
Mayor Daniel has served on the City 
Council for the past eight years. 
He has served as Mayor for the past 
three years. 
Mrs. Daniel's was ap-
pointed to the Planning Commission 
approximately five years ago. 
She 
currently is the Planning Commission's 
Chairperson. 
It should be noted that neither Mayor Daniel nor 
his wife own any property in Needles except their 
residence. 
The case I have researched regarding this is: 
Marcia Kimura vs. Harold Robbins 
89 Cal. App. 3d 871 
152 Cal. Reporter 569 
It would appear from this case that there is a compelling 
public interest as to the decision of the Planning Com-
mission on zoning and the like, which are subject to 
be reviewed by the City Council. 
A conflict of interest 
might exist, or might give the appearance to the public 
of existing, in this situation, where the official 
decisions of a wife were officially reviewable by her 
husband. 


Fair Political Practices Comm. 
11-20-84 
page 2 
The case stated "A Planning Commissioner and a Council 
Member (with review powers) married to each other, can 
conceivably raise a substantial question of fairness 
and bias, prejudice or influence, in the vital County 
planning processes, obvious enough to have an effect 
on public confidence in such processes." 
Therefore, my initial observation is that a conflict 
exists. 
However, the Mayor has requested that I ob-
tain a written opinion from the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
Thank you for your cooperation and courtesy regarding 
this matter. 
Sincerely, 
LOUISE T. CLOSS 


P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
Louise T. Closs 
Closs & Closs 
P.o. Box 1070 
Needles, CA 
92363 
Dear Ms. Closs: 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6«1 
December 3, 1984 
Re: 
A-84-294 
Statements of Economic Intere.t 
322-64« 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an 
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. 
BAM:plh 
Very truly yours, 
.bflttLLtIL (J . //1w}UUL/ 
!Barbara A. Milman 
General Counsel

sections.question: May the Mayor's wife serve as a member of the Planning Commission?
sections.conclusion: At present, our agency is not authorized to render advice on this subject, as the Political Reform Act deals only with conflicts between official duties and private financial interests. For advice on possible conflicts arising from two public offices, you should contact the Attorney General.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Mayor's wife may serve as Chair of the City's Planning Commission when the City Council (including the Mayor) has review authority over Planning Commission decisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks authority to provide advice on this matter because the Political Reform Act is limited to conflicts between official duties and private financial interests, not conflicts between two public offices. The requestor should contact the Attorney General for guidance on public office incompatibility issues.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from Louise T. Closs dated November 20, 1984) and the FPPC response letters (dated December 3 and December 5, 1984). The December 5 response is the final FPPC advice letter providing the declination and referral to Attorney General.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May the Mayor's wife serve as a member of the Planning Commission?

CONCLUSION: At present, our agency is not authorized to render advice on this subject, as the Political Reform Act deals only with conflicts between official duties and private financial interests. For advice on possible conflicts arising from two public offices, you should contact the Attorney General.

The facts are as follows: Mayor Daniel has served on the City Council for the past eight years. He has served as Mayor for the past three years. embedding.summary: The City of Needles requested FPPC advice on whether the Mayor's wife may serve as Planning Commission Chair when the Mayor has review authority over the Commission's decisions. The FPPC declined to advise, finding the issue involves incompatibility between public offices rather than conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act, and referred the matter to the Attorney General.
